title: Pronouncing Ko-So-A-Do and Objects
learningGoal: The learner will correctly pronounce demonstrative words and their noun pairings, focusing on smooth transitions without awkward pausing.
instructions: Listen to the native speaker and repeat the words and phrases. Focus on smoothly linking the 'no' demonstratives (Kono, Sono, Ano) directly to the noun without a pause. Record yourself and aim for a score of 70 or higher.

contentItems.text: When you say 'Kono kuruma' (this car), it should flow together almost like a single word.

targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Pronouncing 're' with a hard English R. It is a soft flap.
Saying 'aroko' due to over-applying the rule for kore/sore/are.
Pausing too long between kono and kuruma.
Saying 'kaban' like 'cabin'. The 'a' must be an 'ah' sound.
Not pronouncing the 'i' at the end of 'to-ke-i'. It creates an 'ay' sound.
Saying 'dore kasa'. You must use 'dono' before a noun.
Putting stress on 'ka'. Keep the pitch relatively flat.
Saying 'watashi wa mo'. The 'mo' completely replaces 'wa'.

expectedPronunciationText: これ あそこ このくるま そのかばん あのとけい どのかさ どこですか わたしも

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! You are smoothly linking your demonstratives and pronouncing the exceptions perfectly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep practicing! Make sure you don't pause between 'kono' and the noun.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Review 'Asoko' and make sure you aren't saying 'Aroko'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: これ あそこ このくるま そのかばん あのとけい どのかさ どこですか わたしも
acceptedVariants: これ あそこ この くるま その かばん あの とけい どの かさ どこですか わたしも
adminCorrectAnswerSet.syllableSplitReference: ko-re a-so-ko ko-no-ku-ru-ma so-no-ka-ba-n a-no-to-ke-i do-no-ka-sa do-ko-de-su-ka wa-ta-shi-mo
adminCorrectAnswerSet.phoneticApproximation: koh-reh ah-soh-koh koh-noh koo-roo-mah soh-noh kah-bahn ah-noh toh-kay doh-noh kah-sah doh-koh des kah wah-tah-shee moh
adminCorrectAnswerSet.nativeScriptReference: これ あそこ このくるま そのかばん あのとけい どのかさ どこですか わたしも
adminCorrectAnswerSet.transliteration: Kore Asoko Kono kuruma Sono kaban Ano tokei Dono kasa Doko desu ka Watashi mo

audioAssets.audioTranscript: これ。あそこ。この くるま。その かばん。あの とけい。どの かさ。どこですか。わたしも。

hintText: Don't overthink the 'mo' particle. It just attaches to the end of the noun exactly like 'wa' does.
commonMistakes: Saying 'watashi wa mo' instead of just 'watashi mo'.
